title: Flarestamina — 40-Day IELTS Challenge tracker is live
body:
I launched Flarestamina (flarestamina.com) — a complete 40-Day IELTS Challenge tracker covering all four skills: Listening, Reading, Writing, and Speaking across 38 active days with 265 tasks. Users sign in with Google, check off tasks daily, and their progress auto-saves and syncs across devices in real time. The tracker includes a progress ring, a streak heatmap, and a class leaderboard.

I also built a gated Teacher Dashboard that lets me add or remove days from the plan, view individual student progress, and track the leaderboard. The whole thing runs on vanilla HTML/CSS/JS with Firebase v10 (Auth + Firestore) loaded over CDN — no frameworks, no build step. Deployed to GitHub Pages with a custom domain at flarestamina.com.

This is the first product I've shipped that other people can actually use to study. Building it taught me a lot about Firebase security rules, real-time sync, and shipping something that just works.

title: Pangeya Essay Platform — full overhaul
body:
Today I shipped a major update to the Pangeya Essay Platform. The goal was to make it faster, safer, and more solid all around.

The biggest change is a real-time onSnapshot listener. Essays now show up instantly after you submit, edit, or delete them — no more waiting around for a full Firestore re-query.

On the security side, I added XSS sanitization, so essay content is now cleaned before it's rendered to the screen.

I also fixed a handful of bugs: duplicate submissions are now prevented, the negative credits issue is resolved, duplicate functions were removed, and stale renderEssays() calls got cleaned up.

For accessibility, I added aria roles, the Escape key now closes modals, and Ctrl+Enter submits.

And finally, some visual polish: a favicon was added, along with a few responsive refinements.

Live at maqsudjon-cell.github.io/pangeya-essay-platform-/index-14.html and pangea8.com

title: IELTS Hub — 73 listening tests
body: Big update today! Added 35 new Diyorbek listening tests — Tests 41 through 76. That brings the total to 73 listening tests on the platform. Each test has 40 questions, full audio, and instant scoring. Check them out at pangea8.com

title: Launched pangea8.com
body: Today I officially launched pangea8.com — my IELTS practice hub. It hosts mock tests for Reading, Writing, Listening, and Speaking, plus the Pangeya Essay platform. Built with passion for students preparing for IELTS, but it's open to anyone preparing for IELTS. Setting up the domain through Cloudflare and connecting it to GitHub Pages took an afternoon. The hardest part was choosing the name.

title: IELTS Hub — 59 listening tests
body: Added 8 more Diyorbek listening tests — Tests 56 through 63. Fixed test ordering, added share buttons, and fixed Test 12 URL. Now at 59 total listening tests on the hub. Full audio, instant scoring, all on pangea8.com.

title: IELTS Hub — 10 new tests (31–40)
body:
Added 10 new Diyorbek listening tests to the hub today — Tests 31 through 40. That brings the total to 37 listening tests on the platform. Each test has 40 questions, full audio, and instant scoring.

Check them out at pangea8.com

title: IELTS Hub — 42 listening tests
body: Added 5 more Diyorbek listening tests — Tests 41 through 45. Full audio, instant scoring, all on pangea8.com.

title: IELTS Hub — 51 listening tests
body: Added 9 more Diyorbek listening tests — Tests 46 through 55 (except 49). Now at 51 total listening tests on the platform. Full audio, instant scoring, all on pangea8.com.

title: Why Pangea?
body: Pangea was the single supercontinent before everything drifted apart. I liked the metaphor — one world, one platform. The 8 in pangea8 stands for IELTS Band 8 — the goal most students aim for, and also looks like the infinity symbol when laid sideways. Pangea forever, Pangea eight. Brand names should mean something to the founder, even if nobody else gets it at first.

title: Making music from Tashkent
body: Releasing UK Garage tracks from Uzbekistan feels strange sometimes. I'm probably one of the only producers in Central Asia making this sound. My album HOME is out on Spotify under Matsumi. Most of my listeners are in the UK, which makes sense — that's where the genre lives. But it also tells me something: location doesn't matter anymore. The internet erased that.
